title: CEL Policy Evaluation Middleware Config
description:
Configuration for the CEL policy evaluation middleware plugin. Evaluates inline CEL expressions against request context.

Two modes:
- **Access-control** (default, no `on_match`): `true` → continue, `false` → 403 Forbidden.
- **Routing** (`on_match` present): `true` → write context keys and continue, `false` → continue unchanged (no 403). Used with the `ai-proxy` dispatcher to implement policy-driven model routing.

expression.description: CEL expression that must evaluate to a boolean. Has access to a `request` object with fields: `method`, `path`, `query`, `headers`, `body`, `client_ip`, `path_params`, `consumer`, `claims`.

deny_message.description: Custom message returned in the 403 Forbidden response body when the expression evaluates to false (access-control mode only; ignored when `on_match` is set).

on_match.description: When present, switches from access-control mode to routing mode. On a `true` result, the configured context keys are written and the request continues. On a `false` result, the request continues unchanged (no 403). Stack multiple `cel` instances to implement multiple routing rules.

set_context.description: Key-value pairs written into the request context when the expression is true. The `ai-proxy` dispatcher reads `ai.target` to select the active provider target.
